title: Add MeshCore direct peer text prefix mapping
description: Follow-up from project-LICHEN-9c4y.12 support-matrix review. Current SEND_TXT_MSG direct-peer path validates the 6-byte MeshCore peer prefix and UTF-8 but returns ERR_NOT_FOUND because no prefix-to-LICHEN-peer mapping exists. Define and implement a compatibility-local mapping from MeshCore peer prefixes to LICHEN app-interface peers, add deterministic no-match/collision behavior, canonical vectors, and native_sim gateway/adapter tests before documenting direct peer text as supported.
notes:
Implemented no-hardware MeshCore direct peer prefix mapping slice.

Design:
- SEND_TXT_MSG treats the first 6 payload bytes as a MeshCore public-key prefix.
- Gateway resolver maps the prefix against lichen_app_identity_peer.public_key[0..5]. Exactly one match resolves to that peer's derived LICHEN IID; no match or multiple matches fail closed with ERR_NOT_FOUND.
- Direct submissions carry the resolved IID through the MeshCore adapter submit callback and gateway app-interface event as has_to_iid=true, to=UINT32_MAX.
- Mapping is compatibility-local and does not import MeshCore contacts, channels, secrets, RF paths, or routing state.

Implementation:
- Extended lichen_meshcore_adapter_submit_text_fn with nullable to_iid and added resolve_peer_prefix callback with documented no-match/collision contract.
- MeshCore gateway resolves prefixes from app identity peers and preserves direct IID in the shared message contract.
- Canonical vector harness now supports expect.fixture for configured adapter fixtures and asserts direct-known side effects: has_to_iid, expected IID, payload, and no submit on collision.
- Added vectors for direct known peer and colliding peer; MeshCore vector counts are now source=38, adapter=32, codec=38, error responses=16.
- Updated MeshCore compatibility docs/smoke docs for public-key-prefix mapping.

Follow-up created:
- project-LICHEN-9c4y.22.1 tracks the shared submit/ACK atomicity gap found in review: compatibility transports currently submit before the OK frame is guaranteed enqueued, and app-interface/message_contract has no reserve/commit/abort API for rollback.
